title: quercus peninsularis
intro: Quercus peninsularis, also known as the Baja fir or Peninsula live oak, is a species of oak tree native to the Baja California Peninsula in Mexico. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus peninsularis are attributed to its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and prevent the growth of certain types of cancer cells. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
